Summary
Follow-up to #22736. Some CI runners (e.g. stage-c-test-8-gpu-h20) ship libprotoc 3.12.4, which treats proto3 optional as experimental and refuses to compile without --experimental_allow_proto3_optional. proto/sglang/runtime/v1/sglang.proto uses optional throughout (sampling params, etc.), s

sglang.title: [KV-Events] Fix kv events events publishing for CP
sglang.author: vladnosiv
sglang.bodyExcerpt: KV events were enabled on every scheduler with attn_tp_rank == 0. With context parallelism, multiple CP ranks can have attn_tp_rank == 0, so each CP rank tried to publish KV events for the same DP rank. For ZMQ this caused duplicate publishers, including a bind conflict on the same endpoint (tcp://*

sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Replace the O(N) List[Tuple[int, int]] materialization with a bigram view over raw token_ids controlled by a new is_bigram flag on RadixKey.
convert_to_bigram_key is no longer called on the cache_finished_req / cache_unfinished_req hot path; key construction and slicing are now O(1).

Backg

sglang.title: Fix segfault in cudaMemcpyBatchAsync on CUDA 13.0
sglang.author: yhyang201
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
CUDA 13.0 removed the failIdx parameter from cudaMemcpyBatchAsync (8 params), but the code was using the CUDA 12.8 signature (9 params) via dlsym. This caused the stream argument to be misaligned — the runtime received a stack pointer as the CUDA stream handle, resulting in a segfault ins

sglang.title: [Docker] Move Rust toolchain install to torch_deps stage
sglang.author: ishandhanani

The setuptools-rust extension declared in python/pyproject.toml means sglang's wheel build requires rustc/cargo on PATH. PR #23014 added Rust to the framework stage, but the torch_deps stage installs sglang first (line 228) and was failing with "can't find Rust compiler".
Move the install up to torc

Reasoning-model requests (--reasoning-parser <x>) insert all output tokens

vllm.title: [EPLB] Refactor Async EPLB synchronization logic
vllm.author: SageMoore
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add two new classes that dramatically simplify the handoff logic between the async worker thread and the main thread.
The first is AsyncEplbLayerResult. This class contains all of the data that the main thread needs to run _move_to_workspace. It is set at the end of each loop iteration in th

vllm.title: [Core] Label torch trace logging overhead with dynamo_timed
vllm.author: frgossen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Add dynamo_timed instrumentation to PiecewiseBackend._log_compile_start
so its cost is visible in torch compile traces. This helps identify
whether TORCH_TRACE/tlparse logging is a significant overhead during
compilation.
Test Plan

The decorator is observational only — no behavioral change.

vllm.title: Update flashinfer to 0.6.8
vllm.author: bai
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Update flashinfer to 0.6.8 and re-enable FlashInfer CUTLASS MoE on SM121.
download_trtllm_headers was replaced with download-cubin upstream.
See also: #39825
Test Plan
No crashes in EngineCore in flashinfer_cutlass_fused_moe.

vllm.title: [Core] Pass donate_graph_module=True to standalone_compile
vllm.author: frgossen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Pass donate_graph_module=True to standalone_compile for PyTorch >= 2.13dev.
This allows standalone_compile to take ownership of the graph module,
avoiding unnecessary copies.
Test Plan
Compile benchmark: 1 repeat, Llama-3-70B, TP=4, cold compile.
Test Result
No regression:

Current: 29.1s (n

vllm.title: [WideEP] Remove naive all2all. Use allgather_reducescatter instead
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Remove the naive broadcast-based all2all implementation and use the allgather_reducescatter instead if naive is specified.
allgather_reducescatter is already the default and should be more efficient. Overall, this simplifies the codebase by removing a seldom used backend

vllm.title: [Core] Cache InductorPass.hash_source with functools.cache
vllm.author: frgossen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
InductorPass.hash_source() calls inspect.getsource() on every pass
class each time it's invoked, which is expensive. Since source code
doesn't change at runtime, extract the hashing into a module-level
_hash_source_cached() helper decorated with functools.cache. Object
instances are resolved

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Gemma4: fix multimodal embedder norm order to match HF reference
vllm.author: lucianommartins
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fix the norm ordering in Gemma4MultimodalEmbedder to match the HF transformers reference implementation. The vLLM implementation had post-projection RMSNorm while the HF reference uses pre-projection RMSNorm.

before (incorrect): Linear(768→hidden) then RMSNorm(hidden)
after (correct): RMSNo

vllm.title: [MM][Misc] Support image+video mixed inputs (per prompt) for VLM examples
vllm.author: shen-shanshan
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
The VLM offline inference example (examples/offline_inference/vision_language.py) previously only accepted a single modality per run — either image or video. Users who wanted to test vision-language models on prompts that combine both an image and a video in the same context had no way to do

vllm.title: [MoE] Triton MoE Perf regression - restore low latency path
vllm.author: milesial
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The refactor in #36286 made it so that the unquantized MoE path taken now bypasses the low-latency optimization introduced in #29354.
FP8 regression was also introduced by #30825.
This PR re-introduces the optimization in the new modular path.
Impact is around 3-4% tok/s on B200 for nemotron nano 3.

vllm.title: [Feat] dflash support for ROCm

Num prompts per run: 8
Max new tokens per request: 128
Concurrencies: [32, 16, 8, 1]
